title: NVIDIA
tagline: Brand-faithful Mode A refresh of nvidia.com/en-us/. Type and palette pinned to the captured surface; improvements target structural and system gaps. Three variants share one direction across two real pages and one synthetic block catalog.

direction.phrase: follow the current NVIDIA design and apply improvements that preserve the current design principles
movements: brand-faithful: palette + type pinned to captured surface
audience: 5 tracks preserved (gamer / enterprise / developer / creator / industry-buyer)
mega-menu: eager render → lazy-load on hover (~85% smaller initial payload)
hero: 8-slide auto-rotating carousel → 1 anchor slide + 4-tile rail
site-wide: real semantic landmarks, single visible H1 per page, 8-verb CTA closed list, modular major-third type scale

palette.name: NVIDIA brand-pinned
brandFaithfulInversions: saturated NVIDIA green #76b900 retained as single CTA accent

originalCritique: The brand surface is genuinely distinctive — sharp 0px corners, single-accent NVIDIA green, two-surface dark/light alternation, sentence-case headings, proper-noun product naming. But the captured site runs an 8-slide auto-rotating hero where every slide claims lead-story status; the page H1 is a hidden 15px scaffold while the visible 48px headline is an H2; 0 <main> and 0 <footer> landmarks on home; 9 distinct CTA verbs across one page (six clustering in the same see-more bucket); a 440-card mega-menu eager-rendered into every page's initial HTML (~85% of paint-blocking DOM is hidden navigation); and an ad-hoc 12-ratio type scale across pages. The strongest assets — the brand mannerism, the audience-track IA, the news-density home — are present but never composed against a system.

variants.name: Home
variants.thesis: Brand-faithful refresh of the news/announcement home. Same palette, type, and sharp-corner mannerism verbatim. The page gets the structural and accessibility floor it was missing: real landmarks, single visible H1, one anchor + 4-tile hero rail instead of an 8-slide carousel, 8-verb CTA list, lazy-loaded mega-menu.
variants.fix: Structural floor — wrap content in <main>, footer in <footer>, single visible H1 per page (the carousel headline becomes H1; the hidden 15px scaffold is removed). Hero rest — 8-slide auto-rotating carousel becomes 1 anchor slide + 4-tile 'more from NVIDIA' rail; no auto-rotation (also fixes WCAG 2.2.2). Voice consolidation — 9 CTA verbs on one page collapsed to the canonical 8-verb taxonomy (Try Now, Learn More, See Buying Options, Subscribe, Get Started, Talk to an Expert, Contact, Play). Mega-menu lazy-load — initial HTML ships only the 5 tab labels + skeleton; tile content loads on hover from a static JSON. ~85% smaller initial DOM. The brand mannerism survives verbatim — the page just earns the system underneath it.

variants.name: Data center
variants.thesis: Same chrome and brand surface as the approved home, applied to a product/solution landing page. Static photo hero replaces the carousel; architecture grid lands in one consistent text-led variant; news rail and customer-stories rail use distinct tile variants so the cadence stays clean. New flow-step and use-case 50/50 patterns introduced for content shapes home doesn't have.
variants.fix: Reuse the approved chrome — header, geo-banner, footer lift verbatim from home. Static hero — single full-bleed photo, eyebrow + H1 + lead + primary CTA, no auto-rotation. Consistent grid cadence — architecture row uses one text-led variant for all four tiles; news rail and customer-stories rail use distinct tile variants so the rhythm reads. New patterns — flow-4-step (mono step numbers, hairline connectors) for the Cloud → Office → Data Center → Edge sequence; use-case 50/50 (full-bleed image + text-block, alternating sides) for the three workload features. Same site-wide gains as home: real landmarks, single visible H1, 8-verb CTA list, lazy mega-menu.

variants.name: Block library
variants.thesis: Synthetic catalog page (no live nvidia.com source). One continuous scroll exposing all 25 blocks (Tier 1 chrome → Tier 5 long-form) in render order, every block at full size, in context, against the approved Mode A tokens. Each block preceded by a label card carrying ID + purpose + content shape + status + source page, so a developer building the implementation or an LLM running the intent-to-layout pipeline can map ID → visual sample without leaving the page.
